steps.explanation: The graph passes through \((8, 0)\) and \((-5, 0)\), so those are the two zeros of \(f\). Write \(f(x) = a(x - 8)(x + 5)\) where \(a\) is a positive integer greater than 1.
Expand: \(f(x) = a(x^{2} - 3x - 40) = ax^{2} - 3ax - 40a\). Comparing with \(ax^{2} + bx + c\), we get \(b = -3a\).
Therefore \(a + b = a + (-3a) = -2a\). Since \(a\) is an integer greater than 1, \(a \geq 2\), so \(a + b \leq -4\).

Evaluating all choices:
  • Choice A: \(a + b = 4\). This requires \(-2a = 4 \Rightarrow a = -2\), but \(a\) must be a positive integer. Incorrect.
  • Choice B: \(a + b = 3\). This requires \(-2a = 3\), not an integer. Incorrect.
  • Choice C: \(a + b = -2\). This requires \(-2a = -2 \Rightarrow a = 1\), but \(a\) must be greater than 1. Incorrect.
  • Choice D: \(a + b = -4\). This requires \(-2a = -4 \Rightarrow a = 2\), which is an integer greater than 1 ✓. That matches choice D — D is correct.
